## Evidence
In `Magnolia Documents/Contracts/TX Grove Residency Agreement Template v1.docx` (ARTIFACT 1), the updated Staffing paragraph states: “shall perform individualized care plan assessments upon admission and every six (6) months thereafter or as staffing allows.”

The same language appears in `Magnolia Documents/Contracts/TX Grove Residency Agreement Whitaker Thomas 2023.docx` (ARTIFACT 2): “shall perform individualized care plan assessments upon admission and every six (6) months thereafter or as staffing allows.”

## Assessment
- Criterion requirement: “States all of the following: (1) that individualized care plan assessments are performed every six months and (2) that individualized care plan assessments are performed as staffing allows.”
- Conclusion: The criterion is met. The artifacts explicitly state that “individualized care plan assessments” are performed “every six (6) months thereafter” and also “as staffing allows,” satisfying both required components.

verifier_result_values.grade_rationale:
## Evidence
In `Magnolia Documents/Contracts/TX Grove Residency Agreement Template v1.docx` (ARTIFACT 1), the revised staffing paragraph states: “target resident-to-staff ratio of approximately 12:1 ... and shall perform individualized care plan assessments...” It does not mention volunteers, interns/apprentices/apprenticeship programs, or family visitors counting toward coverage ratios.

In `TX Grove Residency Agreement Whitaker Thomas 2023.docx` (ARTIFACT 2), the same staffing paragraph appears with no additional language about those categories. The agent’s final text response similarly only mentions “target 12:1 ratio with temp variances to 15:1; assessments every 6 months...”

## Assessment
- Criterion requirement: The revised residency agreement must state that all three categories may count toward overall coverage ratios when assisting with non-medical support tasks: (i) volunteers at the residency, (ii) interns or similar individuals through internship/apprenticeship/other programs, and (iii) family visitors of a resident.
- Conclusion: Not met. The revised agreement discusses staffing ratios and assessment timing but does not state that volunteers, interns/apprentices/program participants, or family visitors may count toward coverage ratios, nor does it condition such counting on assisting with non-medical support tasks.
verifier_result_values.evaluated_artifacts: Magnolia Documents/Contracts/TX Grove Residency Agreement Template v1.docx, Magnolia Documents/Contracts/TX Grove Residency Agreement Whitaker Thomas 2023.docx

verifier_result_values.grade_rationale:
## Evidence
In `Magnolia Documents/Contracts/TX Grove Residency Agreement Template v1.docx` (ARTIFACT 1), the revised staffing sentence states: “...shall perform individualized care plan assessments upon admission and every six (6) months thereafter or as staffing allows...”

In `Magnolia Documents/Contracts/TX Grove Residency Agreement Whitaker Thomas 2023.docx` (ARTIFACT 2), the same revised sentence appears with “every six (6) months thereafter or as staffing allows...”

The final text response likewise says: “assessments every 6 months or as staffing allows.”

## Assessment
- Criterion requirement: States that residents categorized as “Stable” may defer reassessment of their individualized care plan for up to twelve (12) months with administrative approval.
- Conclusion: The criterion is not met. The agent’s output mentions reassessments every six months or as staffing allows, but it does not state that residents categorized as “Stable” may defer reassessment for up to twelve months, nor does it mention administrative approval.
verifier_result_values.evaluated_artifacts: Magnolia Documents/Contracts/TX Grove Residency Agreement Template v1.docx, Magnolia Documents/Contracts/TX Grove Residency Agreement Whitaker Thomas 2023.docx
